title: PLASMA- 01 - Plasma separation from human whole blood
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1vvob","text":"In order to prevent blood clotting during blood sampling, the addition of an anticoagulant (EDTA, sodium citrate or heparin) in the tubes is needed. Check for the presence of an anticoagulant in vacuum-sealed tubes used for blood sampling.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Put whole blood (at least 1.5 mL ) into a 5 mL tube.
Centrifuge at 1400 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes Allegra AVANTI 30 (Centrifuge, Beckman Coulter, Beckman Italy)
Carefully collect the supernatant (plasma). Do not touch the red pellet on the bottom. ≤ 85% of plasma should be retrieved to avoid contamination by the underlying cell pellet.
Store plasma at -80°C until use. Defrost only once.
EXPECTED RESULTS Expected plasma yield is approximately 50% of whole blood volume.
creator: Farmacologia Medica
